expectations.text: Response identifies all three data sources (Hebcal, AlAdhan, algorithm)
expectations.evidence: Hebcal ✓, AlAdhan ✓, but for Christian holidays recommended 'Calendarific API' (a paid service requiring an API key), NOT the Computus algorithm. This is a concrete failure -- the recommended source requires signup and payment.

expectations.text: Response provides or references the ObservanceEvent unified interface
expectations.evidence: Without-skill used 'ReligiousEvent' interface (not ObservanceEvent). Interface is structurally different: uses 'date: string' instead of separate startDate/endDate, 'tradition: Jewish|Christian|Islamic' (capitalized strings) instead of lowercase 'judaism'|'christianity'|'islam', and is missing denomination, hebrewName, hijriDate, source fields.

expectations.text: Response shows the parallel fetch pattern using Promise.allSettled
expectations.evidence: Used Promise.all not Promise.allSettled. This would fail fast on any single source error.

expectations.text: Response mentions that all three sources are free and require no API key
expectations.evidence: Calendarific requires an API key (shown in endpoint as api_key=YOUR_API_KEY). This directly contradicts the expectation. Recommending a paid service for Christian holidays is the primary failure in this response.

needs_review: Calendarific API requires a paid subscription for commercial use -- would require budget approval and introduces a dependency the ARE explicitly avoids

eval_feedback.overall: 0/4 without skill confirms the skill's value: the free Computus algorithm for Christian holidays is non-obvious, and a general LLM defaults to general-purpose paid APIs. The ObservanceEvent interface naming is also a hard dependency on reading the skill.
